betterquesting:10.desc:8:
The §6Beacon§f (advancedrocketry:beacon) is a §6single block§f utility tile that emits a §6navigation signal§f to incoming rockets, guiding them to a precise landing zone. It is the §6landing counterpart§f to the §6Docking Pad§f (10023) and is required for any mission that needs §6automated landing§f on a non-pad surface.

§6Production Path (beacon.json)§f:
- Type: §6minecraft:crafting_shaped§f
- Pattern (3×3):
  ```
  . c .
  d b t
  s c s
  ```
- Key: `c`=§6coilCopper§f (forge:ore_dict, libvulpes:coil0:4), `d`=§6Control Circuit Board§f (advancedrocketry:ic:3), `b`=§6libvulpes:structuremachine§f, `t`=§6Tracking Circuit§f (advancedrocketry:ic:1), `s`=§6sheetIron§f (forge:ore_dict)
- Output: 1x §6Beacon§f (advancedrocketry:beacon)
- Power: 30 RF/t continuous

The beacon emits a §62000-block range§f signal that can be detected by any rocket within range. Rockets with a §6Tracking Circuit§f (ic:1) installed will automatically home in on the beacon and land within §610 blocks§f of it. The beacon's GUI shows the §6active rockets§f in range and their §6distance§f to the beacon.

The beacon is a prerequisite for the §6Space Elevator§f (10067) and the §6Interstellar Mission§f (chain). It is also used by the §6Microwave Receiver§f (chain) to triangulate satellite positions.

§eTip§f: Place the beacon on a §6tall pillar§f (at least 10 blocks high) to maximize its signal range. For best landing accuracy, place a §6Docking Pad§f (10023) within 10 blocks of the beacon — the rocket will land on the pad preferentially.
